title: useState
description: Local state, updater functions, and immutability.

questions.explanation: All three read the same stale count. Use the updater form to increment reliably.
Updater form uses the latest queued value each time — +3 per click.

questions.prompt: Objects in state should be updated by:
options: Creating a NEW object (spread) and calling setter
questions.explanation: React compares by reference. Mutation → no re-render.

options: Lazy initializer — runs once on mount
questions.explanation: useState(() => heavyCompute()) avoids re-running the initializer.

questions.prompt: When does state update take effect?
options: Prints current (pre-update) value — state is batched, updated next render
questions.explanation: Set doesn't mutate the current variable — the next render sees the new value.
